title: Creating Skills
summary: Complete guide for creating Hermes skills — the preferred way to add capabilities without code changes — covering SKILL.md format, frontmatter fields, platform restrictions, conditional activation (requires_toolsets, fallback_for_tools), environment variable requirements, credential file requirements, helper scripts, and publishing.

cli_commands.command: hermes chat --toolsets skills -q "Use the X skill to do Y"
hermes skills publish skills/my-skill --to github --repo owner/repo
cli_commands.purpose: Publish a skill to the Skills Hub
Add a custom repository as a skills tap

config_keys.description: Enable inline shell snippets in SKILL.md
Timeout in seconds for inline shell snippets
Env vars to pass through to terminal sandbox

code_examples.caption: SKILL.md frontmatter format
code_examples.code:
---
name: my-skill
description: Brief description
version: 1.0.0
author: Your Name
license: MIT
platforms: [macos, linux]
metadata:
  hermes:
    tags: [Category, Keywords]
    requires_toolsets: [web]
    fallback_for_tools: [browser_navigate]
required_environment_variables:
  - name: MY_API_KEY
    prompt: "Enter your API key"
    help: "Get one at https://example.com"
---

gotchas: Make it a Skill when capability = instructions + shell commands + existing tools; make it a Tool when it needs Python integration, auth flows, binary data, or streaming
inline_shell is off by default — snippets run on host without approval so only enable for trusted skill sources
required_environment_variables do NOT hide the skill from discovery — Hermes prompts for them when the skill is loaded
Use required_environment_variables for secrets (stored in .env), config keys for non-sensitive settings (stored in config.yaml)
Bundled skills in skills/ should be broadly useful; niche skills belong in optional-skills/ or Skills Hub
full_text:
# Creating Skills

Skills are the preferred way to add new capabilities to Hermes Agent. They're easier to create than tools, require no code changes to the agent, and can be shared with the community.

## Should it be a Skill or a Tool?

Make it a **Skill** when:
- The capability can be expressed as instructions + shell commands + existing tools
- It wraps an external CLI or API that the agent can call via `terminal` or `web_extract`
- Examples: arXiv search, git workflows, Docker management, PDF processing

Make it a **Tool** when:
- It requires end-to-end integration with API keys, auth flows, or multi-component configuration
- Examples: browser automation, TTS, vision analysis

## Skill Directory Structure

```text
skills/
├── research/
│   └── arxiv/
│       ├── SKILL.md              # Required: main instructions
│       └── scripts/              # Optional: helper scripts
└── productivity/
    └── ocr-and-documents/
        ├── SKILL.md
        └── scripts/
```

## SKILL.md Format

```markdown
---
name: my-skill
description: Brief description (shown in skill search results)
version: 1.0.0
author: Your Name
license: MIT
platforms: [macos, linux]          # Optional — omit to load on all platforms
metadata:
  hermes:
    tags: [Category, Subcategory, Keywords]
    related_skills: [other-skill-name]
    requires_toolsets: [web]            # Only show when these toolsets are active
    requires_tools: [web_search]        # Only show when these tools are available
    fallback_for_toolsets: [browser]    # Hide when these toolsets are active
    fallback_for_tools: [browser_navigate]
    config:
      - key: my.setting
        description: "What this setting controls"
        default: "sensible-default"
required_environment_variables:
  - name: MY_API_KEY
    prompt: "Enter your API key"
    help: "Get one at https://example.com"
    required_for: "API access"
---

# Skill Title

Brief intro.

## When to Use
Trigger conditions.

## Quick Reference
Table of common commands.

## Procedure
Step-by-step instructions.

## Pitfalls
Known failure modes.
```

## Conditional Skill Activation

| Field | Behavior |
|-------|----------|
| `requires_toolsets` | Skill is **hidden** when ANY listed toolset is **not** available |
| `requires_tools` | Skill is **hidden** when ANY listed tool is **not** available |
| `fallback_for_toolsets` | Skill is **hidden** when ANY listed toolset **is** available |
| `fallback_for_tools` | Skill is **hidden** when ANY listed tool **is** available |

## Environment Variable Requirements

When a skill is loaded via `skill_view`, its required vars are automatically registered for passthrough into sandboxed execution environments.

```yaml
required_environment_variables:
  - name: TENOR_API_KEY
    prompt: "Tenor API key"
    help: "Get your key at https://tenor.com"
    required_for: "GIF search functionality"
```

## Credential File Requirements (OAuth tokens, etc.)

```yaml
required_credential_files:
  - path: google_token.json
    description: Google OAuth2 token (created by setup script)
  - path: google_client_secret.json
    description: Google OAuth2 client credentials
```

## Referencing bundled scripts from SKILL.md

Two template tokens available anywhere in the SKILL.md body:

| Token | Replaced with |
|---|---|
| `${HERMES_SKILL_DIR}` | Absolute path to the skill's directory |
| `${HERMES_SESSION_ID}` | The active session id |

```markdown
To analyse the input, run:

    node ${HERMES_SKILL_DIR}/scripts/analyse.js <input>
```

## Inline shell snippets (opt-in)

```markdown
Current date: !`date -u +%Y-%m-%d`
Git branch: !`git -C ${HERMES_SKILL_DIR} rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD`
```

Must be enabled in config:
```yaml
skills:
  inline_shell: true
  inline_shell_timeout: 10   # seconds per snippet
```

## Security Scanning

Trust levels:
- `builtin` — ships with Hermes (always trusted)
- `official` — from `optional-skills/` in the repo (builtin trust)
- `trusted` — from openai/skills, anthropics/skills, huggingface/skills
- `community` — non-dangerous findings can be overridden with `--force`
